title: Red Hat Security Advisory: libsoup security update
overview:
An update for libsoup is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: libsoup security update

An update for libsoup is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for libsoup is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
libsoup-2.62.2-10.el7_9.src.rpm
libsoup-2.62.2-10.el7_9.i686.rpm
libsoup-2.62.2-10.el7_9.x86_64.rpm
libsoup-debuginfo-2.62.2-10.el7_9.i686.rpm
libsoup-debuginfo-2.62.2-10.el7_9.x86_64.rpm
libsoup-devel-2.62.2-10.el7_9.i686.rpm
libsoup-devel-2.62.2-10.el7_9.x86_64.rpm
libsoup-2.62.2-10.el7_9.s390.rpm
libsoup-2.62.2-10.el7_9.s390x.rpm
libsoup-debuginfo-2.62.2-10.el7_9.s390.rpm
libsoup-debuginfo-2.62.2-10.el7_9.s390x.rpm
libsoup-devel-2.62.2-10.el7_9.s390.rpm
libsoup-devel-2.62.2-10.el7_9.s390x.rpm
libsoup-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc.rpm
libsoup-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc64.rpm
libsoup-debuginfo-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc.rpm
libsoup-debuginfo-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc64.rpm
libsoup-devel-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc.rpm
libsoup-devel-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc64.rpm
libsoup-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup-debuginfo-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup-devel-2.62.2-10.el7_9.ppc64le.rpm

CVE-2025-14523: A flaw in libsoup’s HTTP header handling allows multiple Host: headers in a request and returns the last occurrence for server-side processing. Common front proxies often honor the first Host: header, so this mismatch can cause vhost confusion where a proxy routes a request to one backend but the backend interprets it as destined for another host. This discrepancy enables request-smuggling style attacks, cache poisoning, or bypassing host-based access controls when an attacker supplies duplicate Host headers.
